steps: Go to https://api.slack.com/apps and log in with your Slack account
Choose 'From scratch', enter an app name and select your development workspace, then click 'Create App'
In the left sidebar, go to 'OAuth & Permissions'
Under 'Scopes' > 'Bot Token Scopes', add the required scopes (e.g. chat:write, channels:read, etc.)
Scroll up and click 'Install to Workspace', then click 'Allow' to authorize the app
Go to 'Basic Information' in the left sidebar to find your Client ID and Client Secret under 'App Credentials'
For OAuth flows, use the Client ID and Client Secret; for direct testing, use the Bot User OAuth Token from 'OAuth & Permissions'

credential_fields.name: client_id
credential_fields.label: Client ID
credential_fields.description: Slack app Client ID from the Basic Information page

credential_fields.name: client_secret
credential_fields.label: Client Secret
credential_fields.description: Slack app Client Secret from the Basic Information page

notes: Slack apps are free to create; usage is limited by your Slack workspace plan
The Client Secret is visible in the Basic Information page at any time (not one-time only)
You need a Slack workspace to develop and test; create a free workspace at slack.com if needed
Required scopes vary by capability — add only the scopes your app needs
For distribution to other workspaces, the app must go through Slack's App Directory review process
The Bot User OAuth Token (xoxb-...) can be used directly for single-workspace bots without a full OAuth flow

test_endpoint.description: Test the OAuth token to verify it is valid and return workspace and user info
